<!-- agent-bridge:managed prompt=agb-handoff -->
<!-- Managed by agent-bridge. Regenerated by agent-bridge. Do not edit below; -->
<!-- edits are replaced on the next scaffold/upgrade. Your own prompts in this -->
<!-- directory without the agent-bridge:managed marker are left untouched. -->

Hand off work to an agent on a peer bridge over A2A.

Placeholders: `$1` = configured peer bridge id, `$2` = target agent on that
peer, `$3` = title, `$4` = body. Run the full path (agb is not on your PATH):

    ~/.agent-bridge/agb a2a send --peer $1 --to $2 --from <agent-id> --title "$3" --body "$4"

This is the cross-bridge handoff path. The `--peer` must be a peer id already
configured in this bridge's A2A config — if you are unsure which peers exist,
ask the operator rather than guessing. Add `--dry-run` first to resolve and
validate the route without writing the outbox. For same-bridge handoff of an
existing queue task, use `~/.agent-bridge/agb handoff <id> --to <agent>
--from <agent-id>` instead.
